nodes.label: ToolRegistry
nodes.purpose: Phase 2 dynamic tool registry exposing getToolsForContext(ToolContext) — replaces hardcoded tool list to make Phase 11 a feature flip, not a rewrite.
what_happens: Registers named tool sets (e.g., 'browse', 'analyze', 'analyze_interactive') instead of a fixed array
Resolves tool set at runtime based on ToolContext { mode, compositionMode?, executionMode? }
In Phase 2 returns 23 browse tools or 5 analyze tools; Phase 11 adds a third 'analyze_interactive' set (9 browser + 6 analysis)
Defines schemas for 3 analysis output tools (produce_finding, mark_heuristic_pass, mark_heuristic_needs_review) at Phase 2 even though they only activate in Phase 11

dependencies.name: MCPToolDefinition
dependencies.reason: Tool schema contract registered into the registry

dependencies.name: RawFindingSchema (§5.2)
dependencies.reason: Input schema for the produce_finding output tool

internal_architecture.summary: A registry keyed by set name with a context-resolver that maps ToolContext to a set, decoupling tool exposure from caller mode. Phase 2 ships 'browse' and 'analyze' sets; Phase 11 adds 'analyze_interactive' without modifying callers.

components.name: registerToolSet(setName, tools)
components.role: Registers a named tool collection

components.name: getToolSet(setName)
components.role: Direct lookup of a registered set

components.name: getToolsForContext(context)
components.role: Resolves the right set from caller context

components.name: analyzeOutputTools schemas
components.role: produce_finding / mark_heuristic_pass / mark_heuristic_needs_review (defined Phase 2, activated Phase 11)

internal_flow: Bootstrap: register 'browse' (23 tools) and 'analyze' (5 tools) sets at Phase 2
Caller passes ToolContext to getToolsForContext()
Registry resolves the requested set and returns the MCPToolDefinition array
Phase 11: 'analyze_interactive' set added (9 browser + 6 analysis); selection driven by compositionMode

nodes.why_matters: Eliminates Phase 11 rewrite of MCP server tool exposure. Without this registry, hardcoded tool lists would force ~weeks of refactoring once interactive composition activates.
nodes.example: MCP server boots; ToolRegistry.getToolsForContext({ mode: 'analyze', compositionMode: 'static' }) returns 5 analysis tools. Later, the same call with compositionMode: 'interactive' returns 15 (9 browser + 6 analysis).

nodes.label: SafetyContext
nodes.purpose: Phase 4 analysis-aware ActionClassifier signature: classifies safety using calling node + browser session, not just tool name + URL.
what_happens: Phase 4 builds ActionClassifier with the SafetyContext parameter shape (callingNode defaults to 'browse')
Phase 4 implements browse-mode rules only; Phase 11 adds analyze-mode rules without changing the interface
Detects whether the caller is browse / analyze / explore for context-specific gating
Passes browserSession through so Phase 11 can do focused-element checks (e.g., is activeElement inside a <form>?)
REQ-COMP-011a: classifyPressKey reclassifies Enter to 'sensitive' when document.activeElement.closest('form') is non-null — BLOCKED during analysis
inputs: SafetyContext { toolName, toolArgs, currentUrl, callingNode: 'browse'|'analyze'|'explore', domainPolicy, browserSession? }
outputs: ActionClass (safe | caution | sensitive | blocked) — sync or async (Promise) for focused-element checks

dependencies.name: BrowserSession
dependencies.reason: Optional handle to evaluate activeElement.closest('form') for Enter-key reclassification

dependencies.name: DomainPolicy
dependencies.reason: Per-domain allow/deny policy passed via context

internal_architecture.summary: Context-aware classification function that consumes a SafetyContext object instead of positional args. Phase 4 covers browse rules; Phase 11 layers analyze-mode rules (Enter-key reclassification, sensitive blocking, navigation detection) onto the same signature.

components.name: SafetyContext type
components.role: Carries toolName, toolArgs, currentUrl, callingNode, domainPolicy, optional browserSession

components.name: classifyAction(context)
components.role: Returns ActionClass — Phase 4 browse rules only

components.name: classifyPressKey(key, browserSession)
components.role: Phase 11 — reclassifies Enter to 'sensitive' inside <form>

internal_flow: Caller assembles SafetyContext including callingNode
Classifier checks deterministic rules (domainPolicy, sensitive tool list)
Phase 11: if callingNode === 'analyze', apply analyze-specific rules (Enter inside form → sensitive, navigation tools blocked)
Returns ActionClass; sensitive/blocked short-circuits the tool execution

nodes.why_matters: Allows the same classifier to enforce different rules for browse vs analyze without forking the codebase, and keeps the deterministic-code safety gate (not LLM judgment) per §11.
nodes.example: During interactive analysis, LLM calls browser_press_key({key: 'Enter'}) on a focused checkout form input. classifyPressKey sees activeElement.closest('form') = true → returns 'sensitive' → tool execution blocked → LLM receives error message and continues evaluation from current evidence.

nodes.label: BrowserSessionManager
nodes.purpose: Phase 4 external session injection — orchestrator owns session lifecycle; browse and analyze borrow the same handle, never own it.
what_happens: Orchestrator calls BrowserSessionManager.createSession(config) BEFORE browse
Stores session.id in AuditState.browser_session_id (Phase 5 REQ-COMP-PHASE5-002)
Browse subgraph receives session via state — does NOT close it (REQ-COMP-PHASE5-001)
Analysis subgraph (Phase 11) receives the SAME session handle for interactive evaluate
Orchestrator decides when to closeSession (multi-page reuse vs workflow-funnel hold-open)
Workflow mode: session held OPEN across all funnel steps so cookies / cart / form data persist (REQ-COMP-022)

dependencies.name: Playwright BrowserContext
dependencies.reason: Underlying browser engine wrapped by BrowserSession

dependencies.name: AuditState.browser_session_id
dependencies.reason: State field that lets nodes resolve the shared session

internal_architecture.summary: Lifecycle manager that creates / hands out / closes browser sessions on the orchestrator's schedule. Inverts ownership: browse and analyze become consumers, not owners.

components.name: createSession(config)
components.role: Spins up a new Playwright context with stealth + rate limiting

components.name: getSession(sessionId)
components.role: Resolves a live session by ID for any subgraph that needs it

components.name: closeSession(sessionId)
components.role: Tears down session at orchestrator's discretion (page boundary, audit end, or workflow end)

internal_flow: audit_setup or page_router calls createSession → stores ID in state
browse subgraph: getSession(state.browser_session_id) → navigate / perceive / act / verify (does NOT close)
Phase 11: analyze subgraph: getSession(state.browser_session_id) → injected browser tools operate on SAME page
Orchestrator routes to next page or step → either reuses session or calls closeSession()
Workflow funnel: session NEVER closed mid-funnel (REQ-COMP-022)

nodes.why_matters: Without external ownership, sharing a session between browse and analyze in Phase 11 would require a full rewrite of session plumbing. This shape lets Phase 5 ship with the right contract for free.
nodes.example: audit_setup: const session = await BrowserSessionManager.createSession(cfg); state.browser_session_id = session.id. Browse navigates and stabilises page. Phase 11 analyze receives the same session, calls browser_click on a tab, evaluates, returns. Orchestrator runs restore_state then page_router decides whether to closeSession or reuse for the next URL.

nodes.label: EvaluateStrategy interface
nodes.purpose: Phase 7 pluggable contract for evaluate node — strategy pattern that lets Phase 11 swap the evaluate implementation without touching graph topology.
what_happens: Phase 7 introduces the EvaluateStrategy interface so the evaluate node is strategy-agnostic
Strategy.evaluate() takes (state, perception, heuristics, browserSession | null) and returns EvaluateResult
evaluateNode picks StaticEvaluateStrategy or InteractiveEvaluateStrategy based on state.composition_mode
Browser session is passed as null in static mode and as the live handle in interactive mode
Phase 7 ships StaticEvaluateStrategy (real); InteractiveEvaluateStrategy is a stub that throws 'enable in Phase 11'
Phase 7 also adds §33 state fields with defaults so static behavior is unchanged
outputs: EvaluateResult { raw_findings, interactions, open_observations, token_count, cost_usd }

dependencies.name: AnalyzePerception
dependencies.reason: Static perception input shared by both strategies

dependencies.name: HeuristicExtended[]
dependencies.reason: Filtered heuristics from analyze pipeline

dependencies.name: BrowserSession
dependencies.reason: Optional injected browser handle for interactive strategy

internal_architecture.summary: Single interface with two implementations — Static (Phase 7) and Interactive (Phase 11). evaluateNode is a thin dispatcher that resolves the strategy from composition_mode and adapts the result to AuditState fields.

components.name: EvaluateStrategy.evaluate(...)
components.role: Pluggable evaluation contract

components.name: EvaluateResult
components.role: Common result shape: raw_findings + interactions + open_observations + token_count + cost_usd

components.name: evaluateNode (wrapper)
components.role: Selects strategy by composition_mode, resolves session via BrowserSessionManager.get, adapts result into Partial<AuditState>

internal_flow: evaluateNode invoked by analysis graph
Resolves strategy: 'interactive' → InteractiveEvaluateStrategy, else → StaticEvaluateStrategy
Resolves browser session via BrowserSessionManager.get(state.browser_session_id) or null
Calls strategy.evaluate(state, perception, heuristics, session)
Maps EvaluateResult into AuditState fields (raw_findings, analysis_interactions, analysis_interaction_count, open_observations, evaluate_token_count, analysis_cost_usd)

nodes.why_matters: Decouples evaluate behavior from the analysis graph topology. Phase 11 adds a new class implementing the same interface — no callers, no graph wiring, no state shape changes required.
nodes.example: Phase 7 prod: evaluateNode picks StaticEvaluateStrategy (composition_mode='static') → single-shot LLM call → returns 12 raw_findings, 0 interactions. Phase 11: flag flips to 'interactive' → evaluateNode picks InteractiveEvaluateStrategy → ReAct loop with tool injection → returns 14 raw_findings, 4 InteractionRecords, 3 OpenObservations.

nodes.label: StaticEvaluateStrategy
nodes.purpose: MVP evaluate strategy — pure single-shot LLM evaluation with no browser interaction. Identical behavior to pre-§33 §7.5 evaluate.
what_happens: Single LLM call with the filtered heuristics + AnalyzePerception in the prompt
Browser session argument is ignored (always null in static mode)
Returns raw_findings parsed from LLM response
interactions = [], open_observations = [] (Pass 2 disabled in static mode)
Computes token_count and cost_usd from response.usage
Phase 7 acceptance: behavior identical to §7.5 with or without §33 interfaces in place
outputs: EvaluateResult { raw_findings: RawFinding[], interactions: [], open_observations: [], token_count: number, cost_usd: number }

dependencies.name: LLM adapter
dependencies.reason: Single-shot llm.invoke with evaluate prompt

dependencies.name: EvaluateStrategy (CM4)
dependencies.reason: Implements the pluggable contract

internal_architecture.summary: The simplest possible EvaluateStrategy implementation — assembles the evaluate prompt, fires a single LLM call at temperature 0, parses findings, returns. No tools, no loop.

components.name: buildPrompt(perception, heuristics)
components.role: Constructs the static evaluate prompt

components.name: llm.invoke(prompt)
components.role: Single call, no tool injection

components.name: parseFindings(response)
components.role: Extracts RawFinding[] from response

internal_flow: Build evaluate prompt from perception + heuristics
Call llm.invoke (single shot, temp 0)
Parse RawFinding[] from response
Compute cost from response.usage
Return EvaluateResult with empty interactions and open_observations

nodes.why_matters: Preserves MVP cost envelope (~$0.20-0.35/page) and gives static-mode audits identical behavior to pre-§33 baseline. Static mode is the budget-optimised default for re-audits and quick scans.
nodes.example: Audit with composition_mode='static' on a homepage: StaticEvaluateStrategy.evaluate() → single LLM call → 12 raw_findings, 0 interactions, ~$0.15 evaluate cost. Total per-page analysis ~6-12s.

nodes.label: InteractiveEvaluateStrategy
nodes.purpose: Phase 11+ ReAct loop with 9 browser + 6 analysis tools injected into evaluate. Implements the full §33 interactive composition contract.
what_happens: Builds tool set: 9 injected browser tools (click, hover, select, type, press_key, scroll, get_state, screenshot, find_by_text) + 6 analysis tools (page_analyze, page_get_element_info, page_get_performance, produce_finding, mark_heuristic_pass, mark_heuristic_needs_review)
Excludes 14 browser tools (navigate, go_back, go_forward, tab_manage, download, upload, evaluate, extract, click_coords, reload, get_metadata, get_network, agent_complete, agent_request_human)
Initializes HeuristicInteractionTracker with per-heuristic cap (2 standard / 3 deep) and global page budget (5 standard / 15 deep)
Runs ReAct loop up to maxTurns (20 standard / 40 deep) at temperature 0
For each LLM tool call: classify safety (CM2), enforce per-heuristic + global budgets, navigation guard Layer 1 (pre-execution) + Layer 2 (post-execution recovery via goBack)
Workflow mode: blocks transition actions (Add to Cart, Proceed to Checkout) — only orchestrator advances funnel (REQ-COMP-023)
Captures InteractionRecord per executed tool with optional screenshot_ref
Token budget enforcement: at 80% of context limit, agent receives 'complete from available evidence' warning
Optional Pass 2 open observation runs after Pass 1 (no browser tools — static perception only)
outputs: EvaluateResult { raw_findings: RawFinding[], interactions: InteractionRecord[], open_observations: OpenObservation[], token_count, cost_usd }

dependencies.name: BrowserSession (CM3)
dependencies.reason: Live page handle for tool execution

dependencies.name: HeuristicInteractionTracker (CM7)
dependencies.reason: Enforces per-heuristic interaction caps

dependencies.name: ActionClassifier (CM2)
dependencies.reason: Classifies each tool call with SafetyContext including Enter-key reclassification

dependencies.name: ToolRegistry (CM1)
dependencies.reason: Resolves the analyze_interactive tool set

dependencies.name: StateGraph (frozen)
dependencies.reason: Read-only — context to avoid redundant interactions

internal_architecture.summary: ReAct loop alternating LLM tool-call generation with deterministic gating (safety + budget + navigation guards) and tool execution. Maintains InteractionRecord log and HeuristicInteractionTracker state across turns.

components.name: Tool set assembly
components.role: Concatenates 9 browser + 6 analysis tools

components.name: HeuristicInteractionTracker (CM7)
components.role: current_heuristic_id pointer + per-heuristic counts + cap enforcement

components.name: Safety gate
components.role: classifyAnalysisAction → blocks 'sensitive' / 'blocked' actions

components.name: Navigation guard Layer 1
components.role: inspectClickTarget — pre-execution anchor href inspection

components.name: Navigation guard Layer 2
components.role: verifyNoNavigation + goBack recovery for JS-triggered navigation

components.name: Workflow transition guard
components.role: isTransitionAction — blocks funnel-advancing CTAs

components.name: Token budget watchdog
components.role: 80% context-limit threshold triggers warning system message

components.name: Pass 2 open observation
components.role: Single LLM call after Pass 1 — static perception only, max 5 observations, always Tier 3

internal_flow: Build allTools = browserTools (9) + analysisTools (6)
Initialize HeuristicInteractionTracker(heuristics, perHeuristicCap)
Build initial messages: interactive system prompt + evaluate user message + state_graph context
Loop turn 1..maxTurns: llmAdapter.invoke(messages, allTools, temp=0)
If no tool_calls → break (LLM done)
For each tool_call: route to output tool / analysis tool / browser tool branch
Browser tool branch: global budget check → per-heuristic check → safety classify → nav guard L1 → execute → nav guard L2 → record InteractionRecord
Output tool branch: append finding, advance HeuristicInteractionTracker pointer
Exit conditions: findings.length >= heuristics.length, or token budget warning, or maxTurns
After Pass 1: optional Pass 2 single LLM call (no tools) → up to 5 OpenObservations
Return EvaluateResult

nodes.why_matters: Unlocks state-aware evaluation: variant selection, form validation, hidden disclosures, modal/tab content. Lifts finding quality without compromising safety (deterministic gates around an LLM ReAct loop).
nodes.example: Heuristic 'CTA visible above fold for selected variant'. LLM observes static data is insufficient. Calls browser_select(size='XL') → DOM updates → browser_get_state → notices CTA changed from 'Add to Cart' to 'Notify Me' → calls produce_finding with evidence.interaction_performed='select size XL'. HeuristicInteractionTracker records 1 interaction; pointer advances when produce_finding is called.

nodes.label: HeuristicInteractionTracker
nodes.purpose: Phase 11 per-heuristic interaction budget enforcement — keeps the LLM from drifting into unbounded curiosity by capping interactions per heuristic.
what_happens: Maintains current_heuristic_id pointer + per-heuristic interaction counts (Map)
Pointer advances when LLM calls produce_finding / mark_heuristic_pass / mark_heuristic_needs_review (signals heuristic complete)
All browser interactions between two finding/pass/review calls are attributed to current_heuristic_id
canInteract() checks if current heuristic has hit its cap (2 standard / 3 deep) — denies with reason if so
recordInteraction() increments count for current_heuristic_id
Fallback: if LLM skips signal, interactions attributed to first unresolved heuristic (REQ-COMP-062b)
inputs: cap: number (2 for standard, 3 for deep)

dependencies.name: Heuristic list (filtered)
dependencies.reason: Seeds initial pointer to first heuristic

dependencies.name: InteractionRecord
dependencies.reason: heuristic_id field stamped via currentHeuristicId()

internal_architecture.summary: Stateful tracker class instantiated per evaluate run. Internal Map<heuristic_id, count> + pointer to current heuristic. Exposes 4 methods: advanceHeuristic, canInteract, recordInteraction, currentHeuristicId.

components.name: current_heuristic_id pointer
components.role: Tracks which heuristic the LLM is currently evaluating

components.name: counts Map<string, number>
components.role: Per-heuristic interaction counts

components.name: cap (2 or 3)
components.role: Per-heuristic interaction limit by mode

components.name: advanceHeuristic(id)
components.role: Moves pointer when LLM signals heuristic complete

components.name: canInteract()
components.role: Pre-execution gate — returns allowed + reason

components.name: recordInteraction()
components.role: Post-execution increment

internal_flow: Constructor seeds current_heuristic_id with heuristics[0].id
Before each browser tool: canInteract() → if count[current] >= cap, return { allowed: false, reason: 'budget reached for X' }
After successful tool execution: recordInteraction() → counts[current]++
When LLM emits produce_finding/mark_heuristic_pass/mark_heuristic_needs_review: advanceHeuristic(id) → pointer moves to next heuristic, count resets implicitly

nodes.why_matters: Enforces focus drift mitigation deterministically (REQ-COMP-062). Without this, the LLM could spend all 5 page interactions on heuristic 1 and starve the rest. Caps make the budget allocation predictable.
nodes.example: Standard mode (cap=2). LLM evaluating heuristic CRO-CHK-014: calls browser_click (count=1), browser_get_state (count=2), tries browser_click again → canInteract returns { allowed: false, reason: 'Interaction budget for CRO-CHK-014 reached (2/2). Evaluate from available evidence.' } → LLM falls back to static evidence and calls produce_finding → tracker.advanceHeuristic('CRO-CHK-015') → next heuristic gets fresh budget.

nodes.label: restore_state + StateGraph plumbing
nodes.purpose: Phase 7-10 state plumbing — restore_state node (Phase 8 no-op), §33 AuditState fields, InteractionRecord / OpenObservation types, frozen StateGraph contract.
what_happens: Phase 7: AuditState extended with §33 fields, all defaulted so static mode behavior is unchanged: composition_mode (default 'static' per §33a; spec body sets default 'interactive' in §33.13 — see note), interaction_depth, dual_mode_evaluation, analysis_interactions, analysis_interaction_count, analysis_interaction_budget, open_observations, session_contamination_events
Phase 8: restore_state node added to orchestrator graph between analyze and page_router — no-op when composition_mode='static' or analysis_interaction_count=0
Phase 11: restore_state implements browser_reload + stability wait + verification (workflowStepRestore variant for funnel mode reloads + verifies cart/checkout state intact)
Phase 10: explore_states freezes StateGraph (Object.freeze) after exploration; interactive evaluate reads but cannot modify (REQ-COMP-033)

InteractionRecord type: tool, args, result, timestamp, phase, heuristic_id?, screenshot_ref? — stored transient per page in analysis_interactions, attached to findings via evidence.interaction_performed

OpenObservation type: synthetic OPEN-OBS-{NNN} id, evidence, category, recommendation, suggested_investigation, source='open_observation', confidence_tier='low' (always), needs_consultant_review=true (always)
GR-005 SKIPPED for open observations (synthetic ID is expected, not in KB); GR-001 / GR-007 / GR-008 still apply

Invariants: analysis_interaction_count ≤ analysis_interaction_budget; static mode → count=0 + interactions=[]; dual_mode=false → open_observations=[]; open_observations.length ≤ 5; every OpenObservation always Tier 3 + consultant review
outputs: session_contamination_events appended on funnel state loss

dependencies.name: BrowserSession (CM3)
dependencies.reason: Phase 11 calls browser.reload + waitForStability

dependencies.name: WorkflowContext
dependencies.reason: Workflow mode verifies funnel-state-intact post-reload

dependencies.name: StateGraph (frozen)
dependencies.reason: Frozen after explore_states; restore_state may consult default state ID

dependencies.name: AuditState §5.7.2
dependencies.reason: Composition state extensions live here

internal_architecture.summary: Bundle of orchestration plumbing: a restore_state graph node (Phase 8 no-op, Phase 11 active), AuditState field additions (Phase 7), supporting type declarations (InteractionRecord, OpenObservation, SessionContaminationEvent), and the StateGraph immutability seal (Phase 10).

components.name: restore_state node
components.role: Orchestrator graph node between analyze and page_router; no-op in static mode

components.name: workflowStepRestore
components.role: Phase 11 funnel variant — reload + verify cart/checkout state, abandons workflow if state lost

components.name: AuditState §33 fields
components.role: composition_mode, interaction_depth, dual_mode_evaluation, analysis_interactions, analysis_interaction_count, analysis_interaction_budget, open_observations, session_contamination_events

components.name: InteractionRecord type
components.role: Transient per-page record of each tool call with optional screenshot_ref

components.name: OpenObservation type
components.role: Pass 2 finding shape — always Tier 3, always needs review, synthetic OPEN-OBS-{NNN} id

components.name: SessionContaminationEvent type
components.role: Workflow contamination audit trail entry

components.name: StateGraph freeze (Phase 10)
components.role: Object.freeze after explore_states — interactive evaluate is read-only consumer

internal_flow: Phase 7: AuditState declares §33 fields with defaults
Phase 8: orchestrator graph wires audit_setup → page_router → browse → analyze → restore_state → page_router/audit_complete
Phase 8 restore_state: if composition_mode='static' OR analysis_interaction_count=0 → return {} (no-op)
Phase 10: explore_states completes → state.state_graph = Object.freeze(builtStateGraph)
Phase 11 restore_state (page-level): browser.reload({ waitUntil: 'networkidle' }) → waitForStability
Phase 11 workflowStepRestore: reload → verify URL still matches step URL (re-navigate if redirected) → verifyFunnelState (cart not empty, checkout form present) → abandon workflow if state lost

nodes.why_matters: Adds the structural pieces needed to flip on interactive composition without rewriting the orchestration graph or AuditState. Phase 8 sets the topology; Phase 11 only fills in the bodies. The frozen StateGraph contract guarantees interactive evaluate cannot accidentally pollute §20's deterministic state map.
nodes.example: Phase 8 audit on a PDP with composition_mode='static': restore_state runs, sees count=0, returns {} — page advances. Phase 11 audit on same PDP with composition_mode='interactive': analysis runs ReAct loop, makes 4 interactions (size XL, color blue, opens Reviews tab, scrolls to FAQ). restore_state runs, sees count=4 → calls browser.reload → waits for stability → returns. Orchestrator advances to next URL with a clean default-state page.
